findings.claim: The integrated MINOR_NOTES note captured only the q1.A trap-label mismatch, but the identical imprecise label persists at q2.B in the shipped file.
findings.evidence: Shipped trap_map: q1.A = "reversed_causality" and q2.B = "reversed_causality". q1's rationale was corrected to '(inverterad hållning — distraktorn vänder författarens värdering)' (no longer says reversed_causality), yet trap_map.q1.A still reads 'reversed_causality'. q2's rationale still reads '(reversed_causality) är den rena riktningsinversionen' and trap_map.q2.B still reads 'reversed_causality'. Pedagogy flagged BOTH (relabel q1-A stance_inversion, q2-B polarity_mirror); the integrated note text mentions only q1.A.
findings.why: Metadata-hygiene residual only — trap_map is not shown to students, the keys (B, C) are unaffected, and every rationale factual claim and verbatim quote checks out. It does not flip the SOUND / MINOR_NOTES / CORRECTED verdicts; noted for completeness because the integrated cross-check under-reported one of two identical residuals.
